tiny_practice.action: Close your eyes and silently reflect on a piece of advice your parents or mentors have given you. Take a deep breath and consider how wisdom from others can protect and guide you.

two_step.reflect_question: How can you develop better listening skills to receive wisdom instead of rejecting correction?

options.response: In conversations, pause and truly hear the other person before formulating your response

options.text: Ask clarifying questions when receiving feedback
options.response: When receiving criticism, seek to understand the full perspective before defending yourself

options.text: Journal about feedback to process it objectively
options.response: Write down advice and critically examine its potential value in your personal growth

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a path with two different roads: one smooth and well-maintained (representing wisdom), and another overgrown and difficult (representing foolishness). Add symbolic obstacles on the foolish path.

reevaluation.question: How has reading Proverbs 13 shifted your understanding of personal growth and wisdom?
options: I now see the importance of listening more than speaking
I recognize how my words and actions have consequences
I see value in seeking advice from trusted sources

three_choice.question: Which wisdom insight resonates most deeply with personal transformation?

choices.expansion: Our speech is a powerful creative force that shapes our destiny and character.
Consistent effort and discipline are the true pathways to meaningful achievement.
Humility to receive correction is the first step toward genuine personal growth.

socratic_nudge.question: If your words are truly the 'fruit' of your character, what harvest are you currently cultivating?

moral_slider.question: How do personal discipline and divine grace interact in personal transformation?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of personal responsibility shifted?
options: I see my choices more critically
I recognize the power of self-discipline
I'm more aware of my speech's impact

thematic_summaries.title: Wisdom and Parental Guidance
thematic_summaries.summary: True wisdom is cultivated through attentive listening to parental instruction and spiritual mentorship. Receptivity to correction demonstrates humility and potential for personal growth.

thematic_summaries.title: Diligence and Character
thematic_summaries.summary: Diligence reflects spiritual maturity and moral integrity, contrasting sharply with laziness and moral compromise. Personal discipline determines spiritual and material prosperity.

reevaluation.question: How does personal speech and character interconnect with spiritual development?
